text:
Market today:
Treasury yields pulling back is a positive signal for your tech and growth-heavy book, potentially easing pressure on valuations. The broader sentiment around tech wealth also remains strong, suggesting underlying capital flow.

Your holdings:
SOXL is your biggest concern, down -38.11% and a leveraged ETF. Its listed stop of 200 is well above the current price of 157.5, indicating a breached stop or one needing immediate adjustment. QQQI is also close to its stop at 53.34, down -5.44%. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch:
Keep a close eye on Treasury yields; continued retreat would be a tailwind. Also, monitor the specific stops on SOXL and QQQI closely.

Bottom line:
Prioritize risk management on your leveraged and underperforming positions, especially SOXL.
plays: Review your SOXL position immediately; with price at 157.5 and a -38.11% loss, your 200 stop is effectively blown or needs urgent adjustment. Consider trimming to manage risk on this leveraged ETF.
Be ready to respect the stop on QQQI at 52; it's currently at 53.34 and down -5.44%.
Consider a new long in NVDA, which shows a strong scanner setup with good bias and discount. Entry 208.55-208.34, with a stop at 206.33.
